original_question:
Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=2\cos \theta \\ y=\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(a,0)$, $(a > 0)$, is $\dfrac {3}{4}$. Find the value of $a$.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=3\cos \theta \\ y=2\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(b,0)$, $(b > 0)$, is $\dfrac {5}{6}$. Find the value of $b$.

Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=4\cos \theta \\ y=3\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(c,0)$, $(c > 0)$, is $\dfrac {7}{8}$. Find the value of $c$.

Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=5\cos \theta \\ y=4\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(d,0)$, $(d > 0)$, is $\dfrac {1}{2}$. Find the value of $d$.

Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=6\cos \theta \\ y=5\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(e,0)$, $(e > 0)$, is $\dfrac {2}{3}$. Find the value of $e$.

Given the curve $\begin{cases} x=7\cos \theta \\ y=6\sin \theta \end{cases}$, where $\theta\in[0,2\pi)$, the minimum distance from a point $P(x,y)$ on the curve to a fixed point $M(f,0)$, $(f > 0)$, is $\dfrac {4}{5}$. Find the value of $f$.
